rulings.Case Title: MARIA GUADALUPE BOJORQUEZ VS. ABM INDUSTRIES, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 8, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS ABM INDUSTRIES, INCORPORATED, ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES, INC., ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER BARBA WAS IN A SUPERVISORY POSITION AND AS TO WHETHER ABMJ-NC TOOK IMMEDIATE AND APPROPRIATE CORRECTIVE ACTION. FURTHER PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE REASONABLENESS OF HER ACTIONS IN USING THE PREVENTATIVE AND CORRECTIVE MEASURES THAT ABMJ-NC PROVIDE AND IF ABMJ-NC'S POLICIES AND PROCEDURES WERE PROPER. MOREOVER, THE "AVOIDABLE CONSEQUENCES" DOCTRINE LIMITS DAMAGES BUT NOT LIABILITY. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. LYDDAN CLEARLY PROVIDES THAT A FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT CLAIM GIVES RISE TO A PRIVATE CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT THAT DEFENDANTS TOOK ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS (SIGNING CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT, TRANSFER TO A POSITION THAT WAS TO LAST A YEAR BUT ONLY LAST SEVERAL MONTHS, FAILED TO TRANSFER HER BACK TO LONGER-LASTING POSITION ONCE BARBA WAS NO LONGER WORKING THERE) IN RESPONSE TO HER COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GENERALLY AN ISSUE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT AND PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO DEFENDANTS RATIFICATION OF BARBA'S ACTIONS BY KEEPING BARBA AT THE SAME PAY LEVEL, PROMOTING HIM BACK TO HIS FOREMAN POSITION AND NOT CONDUCTING A MEANINGFUL INVESTIGATION. DEFENDANTS ABNI AND ABMJ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BECAUSE THEY NEVER EMPLOYED PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THESE CORPORATIONS ARE, IN FACT, HER EMPLOYER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RATTI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 8, 2011, line 15, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Summary Judgment. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT PRESENTS UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ABLE TO PERFORM ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF THE JOB OF POLICE OFFICER (SUCH AS DRIVING WELL AND ADHERENCE TO SAFETY PROTOCOLS) WHICH WERE NOT IN ANY WAY RELATED TO HIS ALLEGED DISABILITY. PLAINTIFF, THEREFORE, FAILED TO MEET HIS BURDEN UNDER JENSEN. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY FAILING TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF CONSIDERING VACANT POSITIONS FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF MIGHT BE QUALIFIED. AS IN THE PRILLIMAN CASE, DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE ENOUGH FACTS TO ASCERTAIN WHETHER IT MET ITS DUTY OF REASONABLE ACCOMODATION WITHOUT UNDUE HARDSHIP. MOREOVER, WHILE DEFENDANT ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE MEDICAL CERTIFICATION OF HIS ALLEGED DISABILITY, PLAINTIFF DID PROVIDE SOME CREDIBLE EVIDENCE OF A LEARNING DISABILITY. DEFENDANT COULD HAVE ASKED FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. THIS RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS BROKE DOWN DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO PROVIDE ACTUAL MEDICAL CERTIFICATION OR BECAUSE DEFENDANT PREMATURELY CUT OFF THE PROCESS BY NOT FOLLOWING UP WITH PLAINTIFF ON WHAT HE DID PROVIDE. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION, HARASSMENT OR RETALIATION IS DENIED. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THERE IS NO DISCRIMINATION, IT HAS NOT SHOWN OR ATTEMPTED TO SHOW THAT THERE WAS NOT HARASSMENT OR RETALIATION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN KISSINGER VS. EPOCA LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 8, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT JUNE GADDIS Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL AN INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAM OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. THE CLAIM FOR COGNITIVE INJURY WAS RECENTLY REVEALED TO DEFENDANT AND THE MOTION IS TIMELY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST THAT NO QUESTIONS BE ASKED ABOUT THE ACCIDENT ITSELF IS ORDERED. PLAINTIFF'S OTHER PROPOSED LIMITATIONS ARE DENIED. THE EXAMINATION WILL TAKE PLACE DURING A FULL DAY WITH APPROPRIATE BREAKS. THE EXAMINER CAN ASK ABOUT PLAINTIFF'S SYMPTOMS AND TREATMENT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TIMBERLY HUGHES, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF VS. CYRYZ, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 8, 2011, line 13, DEFENDANT CYRYZ, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPH 28.1 OF THE LEASE HAS PERMISSIBLE LANGUAGE THAT DOES NOT EXCLUDE NAIL AND MAIL AS A PROPER METHOD OF SERVICE. SECOND, WHETHER OR NOT SERVICE COMPLIED WITH CCP 1162 RAISES A FACTUAL ISSUE NOT TO BE DETERMINED AT THE PLEADING STAGE. THIRD, PARAGRAPH 9.2 ALSO HAS PERMISSIVE LANGUAGE AND DOES NOT BAR A LANDLORD FROM EMPLOYING THE 3 DAY NOTICE SCHEME. DEFENDANT MUST FILE AN ANSWER WITHIN 5 DAYS. =(302/LMG)
